word_list.pronunciation: nˈiːd tə hæv wˈʌnz hˈɛd ɛɡzˈæmɪnd

senses.definition: to act or behave in a very crazy and uncontrollable manner

examples.x: After making such a reckless decision, he really needs his head examined.
Anyone who believes such conspiracy theories seriously needs to have their head examined.
If she thinks he can fix his car with duct tape and chewing gum, she definitely needs his head examined.
If you trust that shady website for medical advice, you might need your head examined.

senses.definition: completely unaware of what is happening in one's surroundings

examples.x: During the important meeting, he seemed completely out to lunch, not contributing anything meaningful to the discussion.
Her mind was wandering during the lecture, and she appeared completely out to lunch.
She missed the deadline because she was completely out to lunch, unaware of the approaching due date.
The manager made a critical error because he was out to lunch and not paying attention to the details.

senses.definition: a person who is perceived as being absent-minded or out of touch with reality
to move clumsily and awkwardly, particularly when one dances

examples.x: Despite her enthusiasm for dancing, she always felt like she had two left feet on the dance floor.
John's lack of coordination is evident in every sport he attempts; he truly has two left feet.
The awkward teenager, with his ungainly movements, seemed to confirm the notion that he had two left feet.

senses.definition: to act in a very clumsy or awkward manner

examples.x: Despite his enthusiasm for woodworking, Mark was all thumbs with a saw, frequently making crooked cuts.
He attempted to play the guitar, but his fingers were all thumbs, resulting in a discordant melody.
John's attempt at juggling proved that he was all thumbs, as he dropped the balls within seconds of starting.
Whenever he tried to fix things around the house, he was all thumbs and ended up making more of a mess.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv ɐ skɹˈuː lˈuːs ɔːɹ mˈɪsɪŋ

senses.definition: to behave in a crazy and uncontrollable manner

examples.x: After witnessing her erratic behavior at the party, it became clear that Sarah had a screw loose.
Jane's sudden outbursts of laughter at inappropriate times suggest that she might have a screw missing.
The politician's bizarre statements during the speech made many people wonder if he had a screw loose.
The professor's unconventional teaching methods and nonsensical lectures make me think he has a screw missing.

word_list.word: a square peg (in a round hole|)
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ skwˈɛɹ pˈɛɡ ɪn ɐ ɹˈaʊnd hˈoʊl

senses.definition: a person or thing that does not fit the current situation or position they are in

examples.x: The ambitious entrepreneur found herself as a square peg in the conservative corporate environment.
The introverted professor felt like a square peg in a round hole during the lively and extroverted conference.
The traditional restaurant struggled to attract the younger crowd, feeling like a square peg in a round hole in the trendy neighborhood.

senses.definition: someone or something that is different from other people or things of its kind

examples.x: Among a group of extroverted individuals, the shy and reserved girl was clearly the odd one out.
Among a team of experienced professionals, the fresh graduate's lack of practical knowledge made him feel like the odd one out.
At the formal event, the casual attire of one attendee made them the odd one out among the elegantly dressed crowd.
In a lineup of identical cars, the brightly colored one stood as the odd one out, catching everyone's attention.

senses.definition: used to describe someone or something that is extremely bizarre and abnormal

word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪv ɔːɹ biː ˌɑːn ɐnˈʌðɚ plˈænɪt ɔːɹ wˈɜːld

senses.definition: to behave or think in a way that is completely detached from reality or out of touch with norms of the society

examples.x: Alex's extreme minimalist lifestyle, with just a backpack of possessions, makes it seem like they live on another planet compared to our materialistic society.
The hermit who lived in a secluded cabin deep in the mountains seemed to live on another planet, detached from the hustle and bustle of modern civilization.
With his unorthodox political views and eccentric lifestyle, Max was like someone who was on another planet, defying societal norms at every turn.
